{"step_by_step_thinking": "Based on the information provided in the documents, the proximal walls of a class II cavity on a deciduous molar do not follow the directions of enamel rods, nor do they undermine enamel which is very thin. The relevant document [3] discusses the technique for reducing the proximal surfaces of a tooth, stating that proximal slices should slightly converge toward the occlusal and lingual surfaces. This indicates that the proximal walls of a class II cavity would converge toward the occlusal surface.", "answer_choice": "B"}
